root_text.iti103:2.1: “Ye hi keci, bhikkhave, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti; ‘ayaṁ dukkhasamudayo’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti; ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodho’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti; ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti—
root_text.iti103:2.2: na me te, bhikkhave, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā samaṇesu vā samaṇasammatā brāhmaṇesu vā brāhmaṇasammatā, na ca panete āyasmanto sāmaññatthaṁ vā brahmaññatthaṁ vā diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharanti.
root_text.iti103:3.1: Ye ca kho keci, bhikkhave, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti; ‘ayaṁ dukkhasamudayo’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti; ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodho’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti; ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti—
root_text.iti103:3.2: te kho me, bhikkhave, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā samaṇesu ceva samaṇasammatā brāhmaṇesu ca brāhmaṇasammatā, te ca panāyasmanto sāmaññatthañca brahmaññatthañca diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharantī”ti.
root_text.iti103:8.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti103:0.3: The Chapter on the Brahmin’s Offering of the Teaching
translation_text.iti103:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti103:2.1: “Mendicants, there are ascetics and brahmins who don’t truly understand about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.iti103:2.2: I don’t deem them as true ascetics and brahmins. Those venerables don’t realize the goal of life as an ascetic or brahmin, and don’t live having realized it with their own insight.
translation_text.iti103:3.1: There are ascetics and brahmins who truly understand about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.iti103:3.2: I regard them as true ascetics and brahmins. Those venerables realize the goal of life as an ascetic or brahmin, and live having realized it with their own insight.”
translation_text.iti103:4.1: “There are those who don’t understand suffering
translation_text.iti103:4.5: And they do not know the path
translation_text.iti103:4.6: that leads to the stilling of suffering.
translation_text.iti103:5.2: as well as the release by wisdom.
translation_text.iti103:5.4: they continue to be reborn and grow old.
translation_text.iti103:6.1: But there are those who understand suffering
translation_text.iti103:6.6: that leads to the stilling of suffering.
translation_text.iti103:7.1: They’re endowed with the heart’s release,
translation_text.iti103:7.2: as well as the release by wisdom.
translation_text.iti103:7.4: they don’t continue to be reborn and grow old.”
translation_text.iti103:8.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
